summary: Merged PR #446 into master. The slice is primarily a Mario fighter refactor: old numbered ftMario source files and address-style names were split into move-specific files, many ftMario_func_* symbols were renamed to descriptive SpecialN/SpecialS/SpecialHi/SpecialLw callback names, Mario state/attribute fields were documented, and assembly action-state tables plus external asm callsites were retargeted to the new names. It also added ftMario_StateVars.h, wired Mario state vars into Fighter/ftstatevars, and added a small dolphin/base/PPCArch.h header. There were no review comments in the provided context.
what_changed: Replaced the old Mario object layout in obj_files.mk: ftmario1.c/ftmario2.c/ftmario3.c were removed from the object list and replaced with ftmario.c plus ftMario_SpecialN.c, ftMario_SpecialS.c, ftMario_SpecialHi.c, and ftMario_SpecialLw.c.
Renamed src/melee/ft/chara/ftMario/ftmario1.c to ftmario.c and changed callbacks such as ftMario_func_800E0A00 to ftMario_OnTakeDamage.
Moved/decomposed Mario special-move code into move-specific source files, including new full files for ftMario_SpecialN.c and ftMario_SpecialS.c and updated SpecialHi/SpecialLw files.

Updated Mario and Dr. Mario action-state assembly tables so callback pointers use descriptive names such as ftMario_SpecialN_Anim, ftMario_SpecialAirS_Phys, ftMario_SpecialHi_Coll, and ftMario_SpecialAirLw_Coll instead of ftMario_func_800E....

Updated remaining asm callsites in ft/code_800877F8.s and it/code_8027CF30.s to renamed symbols, including ftMario_SpecialN_VitaminRandom, ftMario_SpecialS_SetNULL, and ftMario_SpecialS_CheckItemCapeRemove.
Added ftMario_StateVars.h with ftMarioSpecialS, ftMarioSpecialLw, and ftMarioStateVars, then included it from ftstatevars.h and added marioVars overlay access in Fighter.
Renamed and documented Mario per-fighter state fields in fighter.h, e.g. x222C_vitaminCurr, x2230_vitaminPrev, x2234_tornadoCharge, x2238_isCapeBoost, and x223C_capeGObj.
Expanded ftMarioAttributes names in ftmario.h from raw x04/x08/etc. to move-specific constants such as x4_MARIO_CAPE_VEL_X, x18_MARIO_SUPERJUMP_FREEFALL_MOBILITY, and x38_MARIO_TORNADO_GROUND_VEL_Y.
Added ftMarioAction enum entries for Mario action states beginning at AS_FTCOMMON = 340 and macros for SpecialS/SpecialLw action-state flags.
Added src/dolphin/base/PPCArch.h declaring GQR0-GQR7 register constants and PPCHalt/PPCMfhid2/PPCMthid2 prototypes.
Adjusted related Dr. Mario, Luigi, and Ness source/header references affected by the renamed Mario fields or shared logic.
smart_moves: Kept assembly references synchronized with C renames, including action-state table function pointers in both Mario and Dr. Mario tables and callsites in unrelated asm modules that still branch to Mario helpers.
Used the fighter convention of move callback suffixes Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll, making action-state tables immediately searchable and comparable to other fighters.
Split large numbered Mario source files into move-specific files, which makes future matching work easier to isolate by action group and reduces ambiguity from ftmario2/ftmario3 naming.
Preserved address evidence in comments such as // 0x800E2308 and kept decomp.me scratch links near functions, giving future contributors both original address anchors and matching-work provenance.
Introduced typed state-var overlays for Mario rather than continuing to use anonymous per-state storage, while documenting offsets and likely semantics.
Renamed raw state fields only where behavior was evident from use, such as cape object pointer and vitamin previous/current values, improving readability without changing layout.
decomp_lessons: When renaming a symbol used by asm, update every .4byte table entry and every bl target; this PR shows that action tables and item/fighter asm callsites can both reference the same fighter helpers.

For fighter specials, descriptive file names and callback symbols should mirror the move/action pattern: SpecialN, SpecialAirN, SpecialS, SpecialAirS, SpecialHi, SpecialAirHi, SpecialLw, SpecialAirLw plus Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll.
State-var overlays should be added through the central ftstatevars.h/Fighter union so per-fighter temporary storage remains layout-compatible at 0x2340.
Field renames are most useful when they encode both offset and behavior, e.g. retaining x223C while adding _capeGObj.
Do not treat Mario-only helpers as isolated: Dr. Mario and item code can call the same Mario helper names, especially for Megavitamin/cape behavior.
A refactor that only changes symbol names still affects matching/linking if object order or asm labels are not kept consistent.
naming_conventions: ftMario_SpecialN_VitaminRandom for a helper that chooses Dr. Mario Megavitamin color combo.
ftMario_SpecialN_ItemFireSpawn for SpecialN projectile spawning logic.

ftMario_SpecialS_CreateCape, ftMario_SpecialS_SetNULL, ftMario_SpecialS_RemoveCape, ftMario_SpecialS_EnterHitlag, ftMario_SpecialS_ExitHitlag, and ftMario_SpecialS_CheckItemCapeRemove for cape lifecycle helpers.
Use ftMario_OnDeath, ftMario_OnLoad, and ftMario_OnTakeDamage for character callbacks instead of address-style ftMario_func_* names.
Attribute names in this slice use offset-preserving, all-caps semantic suffixes such as x14_MARIO_CAPE_IT_KIND and x5C_MARIO_TORNADO_LANDING_LAG.
Mario state fields use offset-preserving lowerCamel semantic suffixes such as x222C_vitaminCurr and x223C_capeGObj.
State-var types use ftMarioSpecialS, ftMarioSpecialLw, and union ftMarioStateVars.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Retargeted as_table_mario and as_table_drmario .4byte entries from old ftMario_func_800E.... labels to descriptive C symbols without changing the table layout.
Updated branch-and-link comments in asm to the new symbol names while preserving original instruction bytes/address comments.
Used source split plus obj_files.mk order edits to keep linker order aligned after replacing ftmario1/2/3 object files with ftmario.c and move-specific objects.
Kept address comments and decomp.me scratch links beside renamed functions, which supports later verification and match auditing.
Added prototypes in ftmario.h for all renamed functions so assembly/C references resolve consistently after the refactor.
follow_up_queries: Find the original PR #446 for any review discussion or rationale not present in this merge wrapper.
Search later PRs touching ftMario_StateVars.h or ftMario_SpecialS_CheckItemCapeRemove to see whether the tentative state-var comments were refined.
Check whether PPCArch.h was later expanded or whether this header addition was incidental to the Mario refactor.
Look for later cleanups of x2240 and the ftMarioSpecialLw unk/unused/isUnkColl fields.
